calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-07-2020 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-02-2020 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Apr-21-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
Off Calendar Feb-21-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Karyn N. Chandler, Esq. of LEWIS BRISBOIS, T:415.438.6639, for cross-defendant/moving party;
Bryana McGuirk, Esq. of KERN SEGAL & MURRAY, T:415.474.1900, for defendant/opposing party.

RULING: The tentative ruling is contested and argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS SAIFURAHMAN RASOOLI, LYFT, INC.'s Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement as follows:

Defendants Lyft, Inc. and Saifurahman Rasooli's motion for determination of good faith settlement is granted. Settling defendants have provided a sufficient evidentiary basis for the court to determine a rough approximation of plaintiffs' total recovery and the settlors' proportionate liability. The court concludes that the amount of the settlement is within the Tech-Bilt ballpark, taking into account among other factors plaintiffs' claimed special damages, the amount of their initial demand, the parties' proportionate liability (including the comparative fault of plaintiff Kostanze Von Seefried), and the total policy limits of liability insurance available to all claimants from the underlying accident. (Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499.) Cross-complainants Feras Alshraideh and Noor Eissa dba Max Cab have not met their burden to show lack of good faith. (Code Civ. Proc. 877.6(d).)

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
